 {"id":"gt-jpt","title":"Town-level beads: Real DB for coordination mail","description":"Implement Option A from mail redesign: Town gets real beads DB for coordination.

## Background

Mail is now Beads. But currently:
- Town .beads/redirect points to rig beads
- mayor/mail/ has legacy JSONL files
- Cross-rig coordination has no clear home

## Design

Town beads = coordination, cross-rig mail, mayor inbox, handoffs
Rig beads = project issues, work items

Matches HOP hierarchy: platform > project > worker

## Structure

~/gt/
  .beads/           # REAL beads DB (prefix: gm-)
  mayor/
    town.json
    state.json      # NO mail/ directory
  gastown/
    .beads/         # Rig beads (prefix: ga-)

## Tasks

1. Delete ~/gt/.beads/redirect
2. Run bd init --prefix gm at ~/gt/ (town beads)
3. Delete ~/gt/mayor/mail/ directory
4. Update gt mail to use beads not JSONL
5. Add mail fields (thread_id, reply_to, msg_type)
6. Update gt prime for two-tier model
7. Update docs/architecture.md

## Addressing

- mayor/ -> town beads
- rig/agent -> rig beads
- Cross-rig -> town beads","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T19:09:55.855955-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:57:17.032558-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-jvr3","title":"mol-polecat-work","description":"Full polecat lifecycle from assignment to decommission.

This proto enables nondeterministic idempotence for polecat work.
A polecat that crashes after any step can restart, read its molecule state,
and continue from the last completed step. No work is lost.

Variables:
- gt-test - The source issue ID being worked on","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"gastown/test-polecat","created_at":"2025-12-21T22:04:43.420231-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T22:05:10.498762-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T22:05:10.498762-08:00","close_reason":"test cleanup","wisp":true}
-{"id":"gt-jzot","title":"gt done: Notify Witness with exit outcome","description":"When polecat runs gt done, it should send mail to Witness:

```
gt mail send <rig>/witness -s 'POLECAT_DONE <name>' -m 'Exit: COMPLETED
Issue: <issue-id>
MR: <mr-id>
Branch: <branch>'
```

Exit types:
- COMPLETED: Work done, MR submitted
- ESCALATED: Hit blocker, needs human
- DEFERRED: Work paused, issue still open

This enables Witness patrol to:
1. See completion in inbox-check step
2. Verify git state is clean
3. Kill session and prune worktree
4. Close the polecat lease in its patrol wisp

Paired with gt-r6td (spawn notification) - together they bracket polecat lifecycle.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/nux","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:31:31.266716-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:49:20.175392-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-k1lr","title":"Consolidate configuration architecture: three-tier model","description":"Rationalize Gas Town's scattered configuration into a clean three-tier model:

## Current Problems
- Config split across mayor/, .gastown/, and rig/config.json
- Runtime state mixed with configuration
- Hidden directories (.gastown/) are missed by agents
- Category confusion: identity vs config vs runtime state

## Proposed Architecture

### Tier 1: Town Config (mayor/)
```
mayor/
├── town.json      # Town identity (unchanged)
├── rigs.json      # Rig registry (unchanged)
└── config.json    # NEW: Town-level configuration
    # theme defaults, daemon settings, etc.
```

### Tier 2: Town Runtime (.runtime/)
```
~/gt/.runtime/                    # NEW: Gitignored
├── daemon.json                   # {pid, started_at, heartbeat}
├── deacon.json                   # {cycle, last_action}
└── agent-requests.json           # Lifecycle requests
```

### Tier 3: Rig Config (settings/)
```
<rig>/
├── config.json                   # Rig identity only (type, git_url, beads.prefix)
├── settings/                     # NEW: Visible, git-tracked
│   ├── config.json               # theme, merge_queue, max_workers
│   ├── namepool.json             # pool settings (style, max)
│   └── roles/                    # Per-role overrides (optional)
└── .runtime/                     # NEW: Gitignored
    ├── witness.json              # {state, started_at, stats}
    ├── refinery.json             # {state, started_at, stats}
    └── namepool-state.json       # {in_use, overflow_next}
```

## Key Principles
1. **Visible > Hidden** for config agents need to find
2. **Git-tracked** for identity and config; **gitignored** for runtime
3. **Separation of concerns**: identity, config, runtime are distinct
4. **Locality**: town config at town root, rig config at rig root

## Migration
- Move .gastown/ contents to appropriate new locations
- Update all code that reads/writes these files
- Update .gitignore patterns
- Update documentation (architecture.md, CLAUDE.md)

 {"id":"gt-odvf","title":"Document bd mol bond/squash/burn CLI","description":"Create CLI reference documentation for molecule commands:

## bd mol bond

Instantiate a proto into a runnable molecule.

```bash
bd mol bond <proto-id> [--wisp] [--assignee=<addr>]
```

- Default: creates a Mol (durable, in main beads)
- --wisp: creates a Wisp (ephemeral, in .beads-ephemeral/)
- --assignee: who will execute this molecule

## bd mol squash

Complete a molecule and generate digest.

```bash
bd mol squash <mol-id> --summary='...'
```

- For Mol: creates digest in git history
- For Wisp: evaporates (no permanent record)
- --summary: required summary of what was accomplished

## bd mol burn

Abandon a molecule without completing.

```bash
bd mol burn <mol-id> [--reason='...']
```

- Discards molecule state
- No digest created
- Use when molecule is no longer needed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:06.462105-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:20:42.829495-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-odvf","depends_on_id":"gt-62hm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:33:17.530156-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-ogpk","title":"Add neighbor-check steps to all patrol molecules","description":"Part of the 'Gas Town is a Village' antifragility design.

Every patrol molecule should include optional neighbor-checking:
- Deacon checks Witness and Refinery health
- Witness checks Refinery health  
- Refinery checks Witness health
- Polecats can peek other polecats

Use gt peek to check health states.
If stuck neighbor found, can nudge or escalate.

This creates distributed monitoring - no single point of failure.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:16.358314-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:01:16.358314-08:00"}
 {"id":"gt-oiv0","title":"Remove bd sync instruction from polecat startup workflow","description":"Polecats are instructed to run `bd sync --from-main` on startup (spawn.go:634).

## Problem
- Spawn command already syncs beads before spawning (line 239)
- Polecats share rig-level beads via `.beads/redirect`
- Multiple polecats starting simultaneously all try to sync same shared beads
- This causes git conflicts/failures when many polecats spawn at once

## Observed
User reported: 'all polecats failing on beads sync on startup in one run'

## Fix
Remove line 634 from buildWorkAssignmentMail():
```
body.WriteString("2. Run \`bd sync --from-main\` to get fresh beads\n")
```

Polecats only need to sync at END of work (already in steps 5/7).

## Files
- internal/cmd/spawn.go: buildWorkAssignmentMail() and buildSpawnContext()","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"gastown/furiosa","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:52.25177-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T22:21:03.03152-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T22:21:03.03152-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main"}

 {"id":"gt-qswb","title":"bd mol current: soft cursor showing current/next step","description":"Add bd mol current command for molecule navigation orientation.

## Usage

bd mol current [mol-id]

If mol-id given, show status for that molecule.
If not given, infer from in_progress issues assigned to current agent.

## Output

You're working on molecule gt-abc (Feature X)

  [checkmark] gt-abc.1: Design
  [checkmark] gt-abc.2: Scaffold  
  [checkmark] gt-abc.3: Implement
  [arrow] gt-abc.4: Write tests [in_progress] <- YOU ARE HERE
  [circle] gt-abc.5: Documentation
  [circle] gt-abc.6: Exit decision

Progress: 3/6 steps complete

## Key behaviors
- Shows full molecule structure with status indicators
- Highlights current in_progress step
- If no in_progress, highlights first ready step
- Works without explicit cursor tracking (inferred from state)

## Implementation notes
- Query children of mol-id
- Sort by dependency order
- Find first in_progress or first ready
- Format with status indicators

## Beads feature
This is a bd command - needs implementation in beads repo.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T17:00:47.394852-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T17:04:13.388865-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T17:04:13.388865-08:00","close_reason":"Moved to beads: bd-sal9"}
 {"id":"gt-qwin","title":"Refinery and witness rigs should redirect .beads to mayor rig","description":"The refinery's rig clone and witness rig clones should have their .beads directories redirect to the mayor's rig beads (like polecats do).

Current state:
- Polecats have .beads symlinked/redirected to mayor rig beads
- Refinery and witness have their own .beads (or none)

Desired state:
- refinery/rig/.beads -> mayor/rig/.beads (or equivalent redirect)
- witness/rig/.beads -> mayor/rig/.beads (or equivalent redirect)

This ensures all rig agents share the same issue tracking state.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:37.703044-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:37.703044-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-rana","title":"Patrol System: Agent lifecycle loops with attachments","description":"Enable Gas Town agents to run continuous patrols, survive crashes, and hand off work across sessions.

## Core Concepts
- **Attachments**: Molecules bound to agent's pinned bead until complete
- **Patrols**: Cyclic molecules that loop (deacon, witness, refinery)
- **Quiescent**: Agents that sleep until triggered (witness, refinery)

## Design Doc
See docs/patrol-system-design.md

## Phases
Phase 1: Foundation (attachment field, daemon detection, mol-deacon-patrol)
Phase 2: Quiescent Agents (wake triggers, witness/refinery patrols)
Phase 3: Callbacks and Plugins (mail protocol, plugin runner)
Phase 4: Polish (gt patrol status, metrics, tuning)

## Key Decisions
- Attachment as field on pinned bead (not edge type, for now)
- Mail-based orchestration for all callbacks
- Queue replacement for heartbeat delivery (not pile-up)
- Burn and respawn for patrol loops (not in-place reset)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:38:23.416949-08:00"}
